messages.content:
Charles X became king of France in 
 .

The Congress of Vienna led to revolts in France because

it had a liberal agenda that angered the conservatives.
it promoted elected parliaments that weakened the monarchy.
many people objected to new territorial boundaries.
many people did not want an absolute monarchy.
many people did not want an absolute monarchy.

After the French Revolution of 1791 and the Napoleonic Wars, European leaders’ greatest goal was to 

restore peace and stability to Europe.
punish Napoleon for his actions.
help the Catholic Church regain power.
unite Europe under one ruler.
restore peace and stability to Europe.

What is the most likely reason Bismarck was named chancellor when King Wilhelm I became kaiser of Germany?

Wilhelm wanted to reward Bismarck, who was largely responsible for German unification.
Wilhelm did not want Bismarck to have as much power as he had when he was prime minister.
Wilhelm feared Bismarck would turn against Germany if he did not receive a position of power in the new government.
Wilhelm knew Bismarck was popular with both liberals and conservatives, which would help unite the empire.
Wilhelm wanted to reward Bismarck, who was largely responsible for German unification.

The Kingdom of Italy was created in 
.
The Kingdom of Italy was created in 1861.

A challenge that Italy faced after unification was

too much control by the church.
ongoing wars with other nations.
political unrest and instability.
demand for new nation-states.
political unrest and instability.

The map shows Europe in 1871.

Map of Europe in 1871. Existing empires shown are: United Kingdom, France, German Empire, Russian Empire, Austria-Hungary, Kingdom of Italy, and Ottoman Empire. 

According to the map, what was Italy’s status in 1871?

Italy was partially united.
Italy was fully united.
Italy was partly under Austrian rule.
Italy was divided in nation-states.
According to the map, Italy was fully united in 1871.

The group Young Italy formed because Italians were unhappy about

unrest and rebellions.
the spread of nationalism.
the new king of Sardinia.
rule by foreign nations.
rule by foreign nations.

Wilhelm I was declared kaiser of Germany in 
.
Wilhelm I was declared kaiser of Germany in 1871.

Which features were among the six common bonds of a nation-state? Select four options.

housing
history
culture
territory
language

The four options among the common bonds of a nation-state are:

- History
- Culture
- Territory
- Language

Nationalism encouraged people with a shared identity to

declare war on all those who did not share their beliefs.
separate themselves from those who were unlike them.
be open to other people’s traditions, ideas, and beliefs.
convince other people to share their common bonds.

Nationalism encouraged people with a shared identity to:

- Separate themselves from those who were unlike them.
- Be open to other people’s traditions, ideas, and beliefs.
- Convince other people to share their common bonds.

Giuseppe Garibaldi helped the nationalist cause by

capturing most of southern Italy for the king of Sardinia.
leading Italy into a war with Austria to free Lombardy.
reducing the role of the church in Italian government.
staging multiple rebellions to fight for democratic rule.

Giuseppe Garibaldi helped the nationalist cause by:

- Capturing most of southern Italy for the king of Sardinia.
- Staging multiple rebellions to fight for democratic rule.

The map shows Italy in the early 19th century.

Map of Italy in the early nineteenth century. A key notes Lombardy and Venetia are under Austrian control. The Kingdom of the Two Sicilies, Piedmont-Sardinia, and the Papal States are also shown.

According to the map, Italy in the early 19th century was

under the control of Austria.
under the control of Spain.
united as the Kingdom of Italy.
split into kingdoms and city-states.

According to the map, Italy in the early 19th century was:

- Split into kingdoms and city-states.
- Under the control of Austria.

What was a direct result of the uprising in Haiti?

Plantation owners stopped abusing the people they enslaved.
All enslaved people on the island were freed by the rebels.
Toussaint L’Ouverture was released by the French.
Enslaved people began producing more crops.
A direct result of the uprising in Haiti was that all enslaved people on the island were freed by the rebels.

Bolívar had hoped for a unified state in Latin America, but 

he was unable to launch a revolution strong enough to overthrow the government.
instead Latin America was divided into Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela.
instead Latin American was divided into El Salvador, Nicaragua, and Guatemala.
he could not convince José de San Martin to give up control of the army.
instead Latin America was divided into Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela.

Look at the picture.

A portrait of Padre Miguel Hidalgo.

The man in the picture, Padre Miguel Hidalgo, is noted for

stopping the rebellion in a town called Dolores.
beginning the Mexican independence movement.
defeating the Spanish colonial government.
collaborating with criollos and peninsulares.
The man in the picture, Padre Miguel Hidalgo, is noted for beginning the Mexican independence movement.

The term criollos refers to the people of Latin America who were

of mixed European and Indigenous ancestry.
of mixed European and African ancestry.
Spaniards born in Latin America.
Spaniards born in Spain.
The term criollos refers to Spaniards born in Latin America.

Political instability in the emerging Latin American countries was due in part to

cultural similarities.
conflicting views on government.
better education for all.
increased trade and commerce.
Political instability in the emerging Latin American countries was due in part to conflicting views on government.

Saint Dominique, a French colony in the Caribbean Sea where 100,000 slaves rebelled, became a country called 
.
Saint Dominique, a French colony in the Caribbean Sea where 100,000 slaves rebelled, became a country called Haiti.

ook at the chart.

A pie chart showing the population of enslaved people in Spanish Colonial Society in 1789. Free whites and non free whites are about equal at 5 percent each while about 90 percent is enslaved.

The pie chart shows that the population of enslaved Africans in 1789 Spanish colonial society was

smaller than the number of free Whites and people of color combined.
larger than the number of free Whites and people of color combined.
larger than the number of free Whites but smaller than the number of free people of color.
smaller than the number of free whites but larger than the number of free people of color.
The pie chart shows that the population of enslaved Africans in 1789 Spanish colonial society was larger than the number of free Whites and people of color combined.

Augustin de Iturbide was able to declare independence from Spain while also proclaiming himself emperor in part because

he came from a family of peninsulares.
he had started the independence movement.
that was the type of government the Spanish colonists were used to.
he deserved the title after his leadership in the battle for independence.
Augustin de Iturbide was able to declare independence from Spain while also proclaiming himself emperor in part because he came from a family of peninsulares.

Saint Dominique, a French colony in the Caribbean Sea where 100,000 enslaved people rebelled, became a country called 
.
I apologize for the incorrect response earlier. Saint Dominique, a French colony in the Caribbean Sea where 100,000 enslaved people rebelled, became a country called Haiti.
CUNTRY CALLED????
Haiti is the country that emerged from the rebellion in Saint-Domingue (now known as Haiti). I apologize for the previous incomplete response.

Which of these statements describe life after the Agricultural Revolution? Choose three answers.

Most farmland was controlled by the wealthy.
People moved to cities to find work.
Farms produced fewer crops.
More people lived and worked in rural areas.
Landowners put enclosures around land.

The three statements that describe life after the Agricultural Revolution are:

1. Most farmland was controlled by the wealthy.
2. People moved to cities to find work.
3. Landowners put enclosures around land.

What role did crop rotation play in the Agricultural Revolution?

It enabled farmers to plant many new types of crops.
It allowed farmers to share crops with one another.
It introduced new machines to plant and harvest crops.
It helped restore nutrients to the soil so crops grew better.
Crop rotation played a role in the Agricultural Revolution as it helped restore nutrients to the soil so crops grew better.

How would life have been different in the 1800s if the steam locomotive had not been invented?

Products would have been more expensive.
Locomotives would have used wood or coal.
Most goods would have been transported by wagon. 
Most goods would have been transported by steamboat.

If the steam locomotive had not been invented in the 1800s:

1. Products would have been more expensive, as transportation costs would have been higher.
2. Locomotives would have likely used other fuel sources like wood or coal.
3. Most goods would have been transported by wagon, as the efficient rail transportation system would not have been available.
